findings.issue: decision_divergence threshold 0.10 justified by SRE error budget practice: 10% divergence = 90% consistency SLO. Threshold is not set at theoretical limit (0.0 or 1.0) and matches industry standard p90 consistency targets.
findings.fix_requirement: None required. Threshold is grounded in SRE SLO modeling.

findings.issue: eval_pass_rate and trace_coverage lack explicit threshold_warn in manifest; warn threshold derived dynamically in code as midpoint between baseline and threshold_critical.
findings.fix_requirement: No blocking requirement. Dynamic derivation is documented in detector.py.

findings.issue: Sudden model version change: decision_divergence vector captures this as spike in context_class divergence. Verified: same context_class with new outcomes drives divergence metric above 0.10.
Gradual quality decay: eval_pass_rate rolling 7-day window captures gradual decay as cumulative pass rate drop. Signal emitted when below warn threshold.
Empty artifact set: all four calculation methods return safe defaults (0.0 or 1.0) on empty query result. No crash path identified.

approval_comment: Design is sound. Thresholds justified by SRE SLO practice. All four drift vectors have actionable remediation steps. Fail-closed behavior confirmed for all error paths. Ready for implementation review.
